article_meta.title: US News exclusive ICE agent who opened fire on Renee Good was dragged, hospitalized by illegal migrant driver last year

reasoning: The model does a good job identifying genuine bias patterns in the author's own prose. All flagged items come from the author's connecting prose rather than quoted text. The bias type labels are reasonable and defensible. There are minor quibbles but overall this is a strong analysis.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: All six flagged items come from the author's own prose. None are from quoted text or attributed statements. The model correctly excluded quotes from Noem, Trump, McLaughlin, and others.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: Most items are defensible. The 'illegal migrant' flag is somewhat debatable as it could be considered standard terminology at certain outlets, but it's a defensible word choice flag. The 'serial illegal immigrant with a lengthy rap sheet' is genuinely loaded author prose. 'Embattled,' 'sky-rocketing,' 'whopping,' and 'poured gasoline' are all clearly author-voice bias. Most items are solid.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: The model caught major instances but could have noted the slant/framing of the article overall - it heavily foregrounds the agent's prior victimization and danger faced by ICE agents while the shooting victim (Good) receives minimal characterization beyond being part of a 'mob of agitators' (though that's attributed to Noem). The structural slant toward pro-ICE framing could have been noted. Also 'Wide-angle footage of the incident appears to show that Good clipped Ross' - the word 'clipped' is somewhat minimizing language that could be flagged. Minor misses.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: Labels are well-applied. 'Word Choice' for 'illegal migrant' is correct. 'Subjective Qualifying Adjectives' for the loaded characterization is appropriate. 'Spin' for 'embattled' is correct. 'Sensationalism and Emotionalism' for 'sky-rocketing' and 'whopping' are apt. 'Spin' for the gasoline metaphor is defensible though 'Sensationalism' could also apply. All labels are within the defined list and reasonably applied.
